question.text: Rajesh, a shareholder of a listed company, has registered his email address with the RTA. As per Section 136 of the Companies Act, 2013, what will he receive before the AGM?

answer.text: A hard copy of the salient features of the annual report sent by courier
A soft copy of the full annual report sent to his registered email
Only the AGM notice without any annual report
A hard copy of the full annual report sent by registered post

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Under Section 136 of the Companies Act, 2013, shareholders who have registered their email addresses with the company/RTA receive soft copies of the full annual report. Hard copies of salient features are sent only to those without registered email IDs.
**The Trap:** Candidates may confuse the treatment of shareholders with email vs. without email. Those WITHOUT email get hard copies of salient features (not the full report), while those WITH email get the full report electronically.
**Cross-Exam Context:** This connects to the RTA's role in maintaining updated shareholder records including email addresses, which is critical for digital communication under the Companies Act, 2013.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always encourage investors to register their email addresses with the RTA. This ensures they receive the complete annual report digitally and can participate in e-voting more easily, improving overall corporate governance engagement.
